subject: english
source_title: Third Term SS1 English Language Lesson Notes
learning_objectives: Explain noun formation by affixation and compounding
Recognise noun-forming suffixes from the note

sections.heading: Meaning
sections.content: Nominalization is the conversion of other word classes into nouns.

sections.heading: Methods
sections.content: The lesson treats affixation, the use of the definite article plus adjective, and compounding as methods of deriving nouns.

sections.heading: Examples
sections.content: Examples include noun-forming suffixes such as -ment, -ness, -ity, and -hood, and compound nouns such as secretary-general/secretaries-general, court-martial/courts-martial, and woman pilot/women pilots.
